phrases.cultural_note: 'Charmant' is one of the most versatile compliments in French — it conveys warmth and refined taste without being over the top. French people use it for places, people, and gestures alike.

phrases.intent_en: Comment on the historic houses surrounding the park
phrases.text_local: Ces vieilles maisons autour du parc sont absolument splendides.
phrases.pronunciation: say VYAY may-ZON oh-TOOR doo PARK son tab-soh-LOO-mon splon-DEED
phrases.cultural_note: Gratz Park is surrounded by some of Lexington's oldest Federal-style homes. The appreciation of historic urban architecture is deeply embedded in French culture — this phrase would feel right at home in any French city.

phrases.intent_en: Ask someone on a bench for directions to Main Street
phrases.text_local: Pardon, pourriez-vous m'indiquer le chemin vers Main Street ?
phrases.pronunciation: par-DON, poo-RYAY-voo man-dee-KAY luh shuh-MAN vair MAYN street
phrases.cultural_note: Starting with 'Pardon' before asking for directions is essential French politeness. It softens the interruption and signals respect — skipping it can come across as abrupt.

responses.french: Continuez tout droit, puis tournez à gauche.
responses.english: Go straight ahead, then turn left.
responses.phonetic: kon-tee-NYOO-ay too DRWAH, pwee toor-NAY ah GOHSH

responses.french: C'est à deux minutes à pied d'ici.
responses.english: It's two minutes on foot from here.
responses.phonetic: say tah duh mee-NOOT ah PYAY dee-SEE

phrases.intent_en: Say you're just out for a stroll
phrases.cultural_note: The 'promenade' — a leisurely stroll with no particular destination — is a beloved French tradition. Saying you're just strolling is a totally valid and respected activity in French culture.

phrases.text_local: Oh, qu'il est mignon votre chien !
phrases.pronunciation: oh, keel ay mee-NYON vo-truh SHYAN
phrases.cultural_note: Dogs ('chiens') are deeply woven into French public life — they're welcome in cafés, shops, and parks. Complimenting someone's dog is a reliable and warm conversation opener in France.

responses.english: Thank you! His name is Gaston.

responses.french: Merci beaucoup, il est très câlin.
responses.english: Thank you very much, he's very cuddly.
responses.phonetic: mair-SEE boh-KOO, eel ay tray kah-LAN
